summary: A Go-based testing and reporting monolith heavily utilizing AI agents to auto-generate supplementary test suites.
concerns: High volume of auto-generated test directories (glm-agents) polluting the repository structure
Potential test maintenance burden from fragmented, agent-generated extra tests
Risk of brittle tests if auto-generated tests lack deep integration context
suggestions: Consolidate or archive the GOrchestra/glm-agents directories to reduce repository noise
Establish strict boundaries for agent-generated code to prevent structural sprawl
Focus manual review on core logic in runner.go and handlers.go rather than generated tests
